findings.abstract: We present an extraction of unpolarized quark transverse-momentum-dependent parton distribution functions (TMD PDFs) from Drell-Yan data within a Bayesian inference framework, incorporating artificial intelligence at multiple stages of the analysis. Our analysis is performed at ${\rm N^3LO}$ in pert

findings.title: AI-assisted writing and the reorganization of scientific knowledge
findings.abstract: Generative AI systems such as ChatGPT are increasingly used in scientific writing, yet their broader implications for the organization of scientific knowledge remain unclear. We examine whether AI-assisted writing intensity, measured as the share of text in a paper that is predicted to exhibit featu

findings.title: TREX: Automating LLM Fine-tuning via Agent-Driven Tree-based Exploration
findings.abstract: While Large Language Models (LLMs) have empowered AI research agents to perform isolated scientific tasks, automating complex, real-world workflows, such as LLM training, remains a significant challenge. findings.title: The Epidemiology of Artificial Intelligence
findings.abstract: Artificial intelligence (AI) systems increasingly shape how people access health information, make medical decisions, and receive care -- yet epidemiology lacks frameworks for measuring AI exposure or studying its health effects at the population level. findings.title: TIP: Token Importance in On-Policy Distillation
findings.abstract: On-policy knowledge distillation (OPD) trains a student on its own rollouts under token-level supervision from a teacher. Not all token positions matter equally, but existing views of token importance are incomplete. We ask a direct question: which tokens carry the most useful learning signal in OPD

findings.title: Generative design of inorganic materials

findings.title: From Disclosure to Self-Referential Opacity: Six Dimensions of Strain in Current AI Governance
findings.abstract: Governance opacity over AI systems shifts in kind as capability asymmetry grows, and the strongest forms defeat the disclosure-based remedies governance ordinarily relies on. findings.title: $π$-Play: Multi-Agent Self-Play via Privileged Self-Distillation without External Data

findings.title: Enhancing Local Life Service Recommendation with Agentic Reasoning in Large Language Model

findings.title: Seek-and-Solve: Benchmarking MLLMs for Visual Clue-Driven Reasoning in Daily Scenarios
findings.abstract: Daily scenarios are characterized by visual richness, requiring Multimodal Large Language Models (MLLMs) to filter noise and identify decisive visual clues for accurate reasoning. Yet, current benchmarks predominantly aim at evaluating MLLMs' pre-existing knowledge or perceptual understanding, often

findings.title: Large Language Models to Enhance Business Process Modeling: Past, Present, and Future Trends
findings.abstract: Recent advances in Generative Artificial Intelligence, particularly Large Language Models (LLMs), have stimulated growing interest in automating or assisting Business Process Modeling tasks using natural language. findings.title: POINTS-Seeker: Towards Training a Multimodal Agentic Search Model from Scratch
findings.abstract: While Large Multimodal Models (LMMs) demonstrate impressive visual perception, they remain epistemically constrained by their static parametric knowledge. To transcend these boundaries, multimodal search models have been adopted to actively interact with the external environment for evidence retriev

findings.title: "I'm Not Able to Be There for You": Emotional Labour, Responsibility, and AI in Peer Support
findings.abstract: Peer support is increasingly positioned as a scalable response to gaps in mental health care, particularly in digitally mediated settings, yet what counts as peer support and how responsibility is distributed remain unevenly defined in practice. findings.title: Memory Transfer Learning: How Memories are Transferred Across Domains in Coding Agents
findings.abstract: Memory-based self-evolution has emerged as a promising paradigm for coding agents. findings.title: Acts of Configuration: Rethinking Provenance, Temporality and Legitimacy in Post-Mortem Agents
findings.abstract: Work on persona-persistent post-mortem agents typically frames design around a life/death binary. This framing neglects a consequential yet under-theorised condition: when individuals remain alive but have impaired decisional capacity. findings.title: GEM3D CIM General Purpose Matrix Computation Using 3D Integrated SRAM eDRAM Hybrid Compute In Memory on Memory Architecture
findings.abstract: With the rapid growth of deep neural networks (DNNs), compute-in-memory (CIM) has emerged as a promising energy-efficient paradigm for accelerating multiply-and-accumulate (MAC) operations. findings.title: [Emerging Ideas] Artificial Tripartite Intelligence: A Bio-Inspired, Sensor-First Architecture for Physical AI
findings.abstract: As AI moves from data centers to robots and wearables, scaling ever-larger models becomes insufficient. findings.title: Creo: From One-Shot Image Generation to Progressive, Co-Creative Ideation
findings.abstract: Text-to-image (T2I) systems enable rapid generation of high-fidelity imagery but are misaligned with how visual ideas develop. findings.title: Towards Personalizing Secure Programming Education with LLM-Injected Vulnerabilities
findings.abstract: According to constructivist theory, students learn software security more effectively when examples are grounded in their own code. findings.title: HINTBench: Horizon-agent Intrinsic Non-attack Trajectory Benchmark
findings.abstract: Existing agent-safety evaluation has focused mainly on externally induced risks. Yet agents may still enter unsafe trajectories under benign conditions. We study this complementary but underexplored setting through the lens of \emph{intrinsic} risk, where intrinsic failures remain latent, propagate
